Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The True Shape of Io's Steeple Mountain 54 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs 30 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Vector Meson Dominance 32 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. George Goble has died 150 upvotes, 31 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1498 upvotes, 413 comments
  6. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 120 upvotes, 25 comments
  7. NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. Show HN: European alternatives to Google, Apple, Dropbox and 120 US apps 57 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. C89cc.sh – standalone C89/ELF64 compiler in pure portable shell 131 upvotes, 34 comments
  10. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 534 upvotes, 186 comments
  11. Switzerland hosts 'CERN of semiconductor research' 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. LinkedIn is searching your browser extensions 1755 upvotes, 712 comments
  13. Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 798 upvotes, 333 comments
  15. Maze Algorithms (1997) 57 upvotes, 16 comments
  16. Show HN: Made a little Artemis II tracker 112 upvotes, 40 comments
  17. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 260 upvotes, 107 comments
  18. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 256 upvotes, 107 comments
  19. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 290 upvotes, 125 comments
  20. Tailscale's new macOS home 465 upvotes, 234 comments
  21. ESP32-S31: 320MHz 2C RV32IMAFCP+CLIC, 512KB SRAM, GbE, 802.11ax, 61 GPIO 53 upvotes, 23 comments
  22. What Category Theory Teaches Us About DataFrames 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Proton Meet Isn't What They Told You It Was 93 upvotes, 53 comments
  24. A Rave Review of Superpowers (For Claude Code) 23 upvotes, 9 comments
  25. Cursor 3 426 upvotes, 328 comments
  26. Show HN: Home Maker: Declare Your Dev Tools in a Makefile 59 upvotes, 35 comments
  27. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 213 upvotes, 168 comments
  28. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Working on Products People Hate 38 upvotes, 33 comments
  30. ParadeDB (YC S23) Is Hiring Database Internal Engineers (Rust) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 6:30pm, 3 April 2026. Source here.
